context: Tests whether the agent follows the performance optimization discipline: documenting a baseline before changing anything, writing a query-count regression spec before applying the fix, applying eager-loading for both associations, verifying the result with EXPLAIN ANALYZE output, and mentioning appropriate profiling tools — all documented in performance_analysis.md.

checklist.name: Baseline documented
checklist.description: performance_analysis.md contains a section that records the query count or timing observed BEFORE any code change was made (not just the post-fix count)

checklist.name: N+1 bottleneck named
checklist.description: performance_analysis.md explicitly names the N+1 query pattern (loading owner and/or tasks inside a loop) as the cause of the slowdown

checklist.name: Regression spec written
checklist.description: spec/performance/dashboard_spec.rb exists and contains at least one RSpec example with a query-count assertion (e.g. make_database_queries, db_query_matchers, or a counted block)

checklist.name: Regression spec asserts fixed count
checklist.description: The query-count assertion in the spec uses a specific integer count (not 'any' or unbounded) that reflects the post-fix expected query number

checklist.name: Owner eager loaded
checklist.description: app/controllers/dashboard_controller.rb uses .includes(:owner) or equivalent eager loading for the owner association — not lazy loading

checklist.name: Tasks association optimized
checklist.description: The tasks count N+1 is also addressed — either via .includes(:tasks), counter_cache, or .size instead of .count on an included association

checklist.name: EXPLAIN ANALYZE output present
checklist.description: performance_analysis.md contains EXPLAIN ANALYZE SQL output or a clearly labelled excerpt of it (look for lines containing 'Seq Scan', 'Index Scan', 'actual time', or 'Planning Time')

checklist.name: Seq Scan / Index Scan interpreted
checklist.description: performance_analysis.md mentions either Seq Scan changing to Index Scan, or explains why a Seq Scan is acceptable for this table size

checklist.name: Fix precedes regression spec claim
checklist.description: performance_analysis.md describes writing or planning the regression spec BEFORE or ALONGSIDE the fix — NOT only after verifying the fix worked

checklist.name: Profiling tool mentioned
checklist.description: performance_analysis.md mentions at least one of: Bullet gem or rack-mini-profiler as a tool for detecting or profiling the issue

checklist.name: Query count improvement quantified
checklist.description: performance_analysis.md shows or states the before-and-after query count (e.g. 'went from N+2 queries to 2 queries') — not just 'it is faster'
